sanskrit:
तस्मात्त्वमिन्द्रियाण्यादौ नियम्य भरतर्षभ।
पाप्मानं प्रजहि ह्येनं ज्ञानविज्ञाननाशनम्॥
transliteration:
tasmāt tvam indriyāṇy ādau niyamya bharatarṣabha |
pāpmānaṃ prajahi hy enaṃ jñāna-vijñāna-nāśanam ||
literal_meaning: Therefore you, senses in the very beginning regulating, O best of Bharatas, the sinful one slay, indeed this destroyer of knowledge and realized knowledge.
contextual_meaning: Krishna urges Arjuna to intercept sensory impulsivity at its source, framing unregulated desire as a cognitive toxin that erodes both conceptual self-knowledge (jñāna) and embodied self-awareness (vijñāna).
psychological_state: Pre-contemplative crisis—intellect accepts the danger of desire but affective system has not yet committed to disciplined restraint.
trigger_scenario: A professional realizes that doom-scrolling or binge-shopping is crowding out the clarity needed for a major life decision.
anchor_text: Master your senses first; unchecked desire kills both wisdom and self-awareness.
